acceptance_targets: [chapter_15_f01] Pass if a cold reader of the revised snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:137 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:155 can state that Burt is now only Earl's diminished roommate, not an active contributor to the ring's inventory. Fail if any sentence still implies Burt's current prescriptions are being diverted.

[chapter_15_f02] Pass if the revised chapter still makes the pension math, VA lie, cemetery handoff, and concealment legible, but the sections corresponding to snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:41-49 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:163-169 each add a fresh concrete consequence, memory, or decision instead of restating what the reader already knows. Fail if those spans still function mainly as abstract certification of Denise's need or Earl's silence.

[chapter_15_f03] Pass if a cold reader of the revised snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:153-156 can identify which part of Earl's reasoning is known consequence and which part is projection, and can answer what diagnosis would concretely threaten tomorrow or next week. Fail if the text still treats Denise losing the $400 as an unqualified certainty without showing why the diagnosis would actually interrupt Earl's supply role.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if a cold reader of the revised snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:137 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:155 can state that Burt is now only Earl's diminished roommate, not an active contributor to the ring's inventory. Fail if any sentence still implies Burt's current prescriptions are being diverted.
findings.problem: The chapter reintroduces Burt as an active medication source for the ring ('Burt didn't know anymore what his pills were being used for'), which contradicts the continuity sheet's chapter-11 canon that Agnes pulled Burt's prescriptions from diversion once he lost capacity. This is a continuity break and it also weakens Agnes's established ethical line around Burt.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the operational summary at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:137 so Burt is no longer described as a current supplier. If the inventory logic at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:23 or snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:155 is meant to rely on Earl's own medications or another source, make that source explicit there instead of implying Burt's pills are still in circulation.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised chapter still makes the pension math, VA lie, cemetery handoff, and concealment legible, but the sections corresponding to snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:41-49 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:163-169 each add a fresh concrete consequence, memory, or decision instead of restating what the reader already knows. Fail if those spans still function mainly as abstract certification of Denise's need or Earl's silence.
findings.problem: The chapter keeps translating stakes that are already legible instead of trusting the scene work. The van ride pauses for a long household-budget explanation after the pension math is clear, and the late section returns to abstractly paraphrasing Earl and Maryann's silence dynamic after the grave scene has already landed the key omission at lines 119-123. That recurrence blunts the elegiac chapter's precision and keeps it from feeling shortlist-level compressed.
findings.rewrite_direction: Tighten the explanatory blocks at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:41-49 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:163-169. Keep the must-land facts (patient-responsibility math, the $400 to Denise, the VA lie, the no-ring/no-cough omission), but either compress them into fewer, sharper material details or replace the abstract summary with one concrete memory/action that changes the pressure after snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:119-123 has already made Earl's concealment clear.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if a cold reader of the revised snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:153-156 can identify which part of Earl's reasoning is known consequence and which part is projection, and can answer what diagnosis would concretely threaten tomorrow or next week. Fail if the text still treats Denise losing the $400 as an unqualified certainty without showing why the diagnosis would actually interrupt Earl's supply role.
findings.problem: The chapter asks the reader to accept a long causal chain as settled fact: if Earl reports the cough, diagnostics follow; if diagnostics follow, his medications will change; if his medications change, the ring loses a supplier; if the ring loses that supply, Denise loses the $400. Because this chain carries the chapter's decision beat, the page needs to distinguish what Earl actually knows from what he is projecting in fear. As written, the logic reads partly as plot necessity rather than fully earned on-page reasoning.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:153-156, either ground the medication-risk chain in a specific facility or medical reality Earl knows (for example, med reconciliation after a new diagnosis, transfer risk, or prior precedent) or explicitly mark the downstream collapse as Earl's worst-case inference rather than objective certainty. Keep the refusal, but make the operational logic legible enough that a reader can separate fact from fear.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This is a dark comedy about elderly agency, institutional failure, and moral compromise in a setting most fiction treats as a punchline or a tragedy but never both. The risks the novel must take: render the comedy of octogenarians as pharmaceutical distributors without condescending to them or turning them into lovable rogues; make Carmen's escalating complicity fully legible without moralizing about it; show the bodily realities of aging — incontinence, tremor, cognitive decline, falls, the smell of a body that doesn't leave a building — with unsentimental specificity; render the economic desperation of both healthcare workers and uninsured buyers without turning either into social-issue abstractions; give the moral ambiguity of the ring genuine weight by refusing to resolve it into either 'they were wrong' or 'they were heroes.' The novel must trust readers to sit with complexity. When in doubt between too raw and too safe, choose raw. A nursing home that smells like potpourri is a nursing home written by someone who's never been in one.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism for bodily functions, aging, economic shame, institutional dysfunction, or the realities of nursing home care is a craft failure. The novel's comedy and humanity depend on rendering what polite fiction glosses over: what it smells like to clean someone who can't clean themselves, what it costs to count coins for a bus fare, what oxycodone sells for per milligram on the secondary market, what a concussion looks like on an 88-year-old skull, what it sounds like when someone with Parkinson's tries to hold a coffee cup steady, what Burt's dementia actually looks like up close. If the prose flinches from these specifics, the novel loses its authority.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the default for all dialogue. Uncontracted forms appear only for emphasis, character-specific formality (Agnes's low contraction level), Carmen under extreme stress reverting to formal speech, or deliberate register choices. In a contemporary New Jersey nursing home, stiff diction is the anomaly, not the norm.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate high-intensity scenes with reflective interiority. Vary scene count: single-scene chapters for maximum compression, three-scene chapters for range. Comic chapters (3, 9) should feel structurally different from quiet character studies (7, 8, 12, 15), which should feel different from crisis chapters (11, 16, 17). Follow a confrontation with a reflection, an escalation with a domestic scene, a comic set piece with an elegiac one. If two consecutive chapters share the same rhythm, one needs redesigning.
prose_style_profile.diction: Grounded and specific. Drug names (oxycodone, alprazolam, hydroxychloroquine), nursing procedures (med pass, pill count, charting, fall assessment), body realities (incontinence, tremor, laceration, scalp bleed). No purple prose, no literary performance. The style should feel like someone who works with their hands is telling you the truth about what she saw.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, varying by POV character. Carmen chapters are warm and sensory, anchored in the body and the immediate environment. Harry chapters are precise and calculating, with attention to numbers and angles. Agnes chapters are clinical and compressed. Lola chapters are winding and emotionally layered, with embedded memories. Earl chapters are spare and weighted, with silence doing as much work as language. Marcus chapters are kinetic, with physical movement and code-switching. Tammy chapters are flat and observational, her trained eye cataloguing what doesn't add up.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Varied by chapter and POV. Carmen chapters favor medium sentences with occasional long runs when she's anxious. Harry chapters are short and clipped. Comic set pieces (chapters 3 and 9) move between fast inventory and slow domestic beats. Quiet chapters (7, 12, 15) slow down and let space onto the page. No chapter should feel rhythmically identical to the one before it.
